records.ety: [F. draciner; pref. d- (L. dis) + racine root, fr. an assumed LL. radicina, fr. L. radix, radicis, root.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deracinated (d*rs"*nā`td); p. pr. & vb. n. Deracinating (d*rs"*nā`tng).]

defs: To pluck up by the roots; to extirpate.
quotes: While that the colter rusts That should deracinate such savagery.
